key_insight: A Lagrangian trajectory-based integration can reconstruct polymeric stress from known steady velocity fields without solving the full Eulerian constitutive transport equation.
engineering_relevance: This enables practical stress mapping in viscoelastic flows, including from experimental velocity measurements, with lower computational cost and improved robustness.
method: Integrate deformation-gradient history along fluid-element trajectories in a steady velocity field.
Use the history to reconstruct polymeric stress for FENE-P and Oldroyd-B models.
Apply and validate the scheme on canonical flows and channel flows past circular obstacles.
evidence_signal: The reconstructed stress fields agree with traditional Eulerian reference solutions in both numerical simulations and microfluidic experiments.
watch_item: The method assumes a known steady velocity field, so accuracy may degrade for strongly unsteady flows or if experimental velocity data are noisy or incomplete.
paper_title: Lagrangian evaluation of polymeric stress in viscoelastic fluids
